apps.name: Firefox
apps.description: Web browser from Mozilla

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/firefox

debian.install: sudo install -d -m 0755 /etc/apt/keyrings && wget -qO- https://packages.mozilla.org/apt/repo-signing-key.gpg | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/packages.mozilla.org.asc > /dev/null && echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/packages.mozilla.org.asc] https://packages.mozilla.org/apt mozilla main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/mozilla.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y firefox
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade firefox

apps.notes: Free / open source (MPL 2.0).

apps.name: Google Chrome
apps.description: Web browser from Google

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/googlechrome

debian.install: wget -qO- https://dl.google.com/linux/linux_signing_key.pub | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/google-chrome.gpg && echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/google-chrome.gpg] https://dl.google.com/linux/chrome/deb/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/google-chrome.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y google-chrome-stable
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade google-chrome-stable

fedora.install:
sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/google-chrome.repo > /dev/null <<'EOF'
[google-chrome]
name=google-chrome
baseurl=https://dl.google.com/linux/chrome/rpm/stable/x86_64
enabled=1
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://dl.google.com/linux/linux_signing_key.pub
EOF
sudo dnf install -y google-chrome-stable

apps.notes: Proprietary freeware; uses a Google-hosted update repo on Linux.

apps.name: Microsoft Edge
apps.description: Web browser from Microsoft

debian.install: curl -fsSL https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/microsoft.gpg && echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/microsoft.gpg] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/edge stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/microsoft-edge.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y microsoft-edge-stable
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade microsoft-edge-stable

fedora.install: sudo rpm --import https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc && sudo dnf config-manager --add-repo https://packages.microsoft.com/yumrepos/edge && sudo dnf install -y microsoft-edge-stable

apps.notes: Proprietary freeware; Chromium-based; ships preinstalled on Windows 10/11.

apps.name: Opera
apps.description: Web browser from Opera Software

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/opera

debian.install: wget -qO- https://deb.opera.com/archive.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/opera.gpg && echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/opera.gpg] https://deb.opera.com/opera-stable/ stable non-free" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/opera-stable.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y opera-stable
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade opera-stable

fedora.install:
sudo rpm --import https://rpm.opera.com/rpmrepo.key && sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/opera.repo > /dev/null <<'EOF'
[opera]
name=Opera packages
type=rpm-md
baseurl=https://rpm.opera.com/rpm
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://rpm.opera.com/rpmrepo.key
EOF
sudo dnf install -y opera-stable

apps.notes: Proprietary freeware; Chromium-based.

apps.name: Tor Browser
apps.description: Tor Browser bundle for anonymous browsing

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/tor-browser

debian.install: sudo apt install -y torbrowser-launcher

apps.notes: Free / open source; based on Firefox ESR + Tor.

apps.name: Brave Browser
apps.description: Privacy-focused web browser

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/brave

debian.install: sudo curl -fsSLo /etc/apt/keyrings/brave-browser-archive-keyring.gpg https://brave-browser-apt-release.s3.brave.com/brave-browser-archive-keyring.gpg && echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/brave-browser-archive-keyring.gpg] https://brave-browser-apt-release.s3.brave.com/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/brave-browser-release.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y brave-browser
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade brave-browser

fedora.install: sudo dnf install -y dnf-plugins-core && sudo dnf config-manager --add-repo https://brave-browser-rpm-release.s3.brave.com/brave-browser.repo && sudo rpm --import https://brave-browser-rpm-release.s3.brave.com/brave-core.asc && sudo dnf install -y brave-browser

apps.notes: Free / open source (MPL 2.0); Chromium-based.

apps.name: Vivaldi
apps.description: Highly customizable web browser

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/vivaldi

debian.install: wget -qO- https://repo.vivaldi.com/archive/linux_signing_key.pub | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/vivaldi.gpg && echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/vivaldi.gpg] https://repo.vivaldi.com/archive/deb/ stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vivaldi.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y vivaldi-stable
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade vivaldi-stable

fedora.install: sudo dnf config-manager --add-repo https://repo.vivaldi.com/archive/vivaldi-fedora.repo && sudo dnf install -y vivaldi-stable

apps.notes: Proprietary freeware (partly open); Chromium-based.

apps.name: Waterfox
apps.description: Privacy-focused Firefox-based web browser
apps.notes: Free / open source (MPL 2.0); fork of Firefox. No official Linux repo/Flatpak/Snap; use official tarball.

apps.name: Microsoft Teams
apps.description: Team collaboration, chat, and video meetings from Microsoft
apps.notes: Proprietary; native Linux client discontinued by Microsoft; Linux uses the PWA or web client.

apps.name: Signal
apps.description: Private encrypted messenger

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/signal

debian.install: wget -qO- https://updates.signal.org/desktop/apt/keys.asc | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/signal-desktop-keyring.gpg && echo "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/signal-desktop-keyring.gpg] https://updates.signal.org/desktop/apt xenial main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/signal-xenial.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y signal-desktop
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade signal-desktop

apps.notes: Free / open source (AGPL/GPL); desktop links to a phone account.

apps.name: Telegram
apps.description: Cloud-based messaging app focused on speed and security

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/telegram

debian.install: sudo apt install -y telegram-desktop

apps.notes: Free; macOS cask telegram is the native macOS client. Distro packages are community-maintained.

apps.name: Thunderbird
apps.description: Email, calendar, and chat client from Mozilla

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/thunderbird

debian.install: sudo apt install -y thunderbird
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade thunderbird

apps.notes: Free / open source (MPL 2.0).

apps.name: WhatsApp
apps.description: WhatsApp messenger desktop client
apps.notes: Proprietary; no official Linux desktop client. Use WhatsApp Web.

apps.name: Slack
apps.description: Team messaging and collaboration app

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/slack

debian.install: wget -O /tmp/slack.deb "https://slack.com/downloads/instructions/linux?ddl=1&build=deb" && sudo apt install -y /tmp/slack.deb

fedora.install: sudo dnf install -y https://downloads.slack-edge.com/desktop-releases/linux/x64/$(curl -s https://slack.com/release-notes/linux | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+\.\d+' | head -1)/slack-$(curl -s https://slack.com/release-notes/linux | grep -oP '\d+\.\d+\.\d+' | head -1)-0.1.el8.x86_64.rpm

apps.notes: Proprietary freemium. Official .deb/.rpm downloads but no maintained apt/dnf repo.

apps.name: Google Drive
apps.description: Google Drive for desktop — sync and stream files from Google Drive
apps.notes: Proprietary; no official Linux client. Use rclone, Insync, or GNOME Online Accounts.

apps.name: Dropbox
apps.description: Cloud file storage and synchronization client

debian.install: sudo apt install -y nautilus-dropbox
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade nautilus-dropbox

apps.notes: Proprietary freemium; Linux daemon syncs to ~/Dropbox. No official Flatpak/Snap.

apps.name: IntelliJ IDEA
apps.description: Java and Kotlin IDE by JetBrains (Ultimate edition cask)

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/idea-ultimate

apps.notes: Community (intellij-idea-ce / extras/idea / intellij-idea-community) is free/open-source; Ultimate is commercial.

apps.name: PyCharm
apps.description: Python IDE by JetBrains (Professional edition cask)

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/pycharm-professional

apps.notes: Community (pycharm-ce / extras/pycharm / pycharm-community) is free/open-source; Professional is commercial.

apps.name: PhpStorm
apps.description: PHP IDE by JetBrains

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/phpstorm

apps.notes: Commercial (paid/subscription).

apps.name: GoLand
apps.description: Go IDE by JetBrains

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/goland

apps.notes: Commercial (paid/subscription).

apps.name: Rider
apps.description: .NET and game-dev IDE by JetBrains

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/rider

apps.notes: Free for non-commercial use; commercial use requires a paid license.

apps.name: CLion
apps.description: C and C++ IDE by JetBrains

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/clion

apps.notes: Free for non-commercial use; commercial use requires a paid license.

apps.name: RustRover
apps.description: Rust IDE by JetBrains

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/rustrover

apps.notes: Free for non-commercial use; commercial use requires a license. No maintained Chocolatey package.

apps.name: RubyMine
apps.description: Ruby and Rails IDE by JetBrains

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/rubymine

apps.notes: Commercial (paid/subscription).

apps.name: Visual Studio Code
apps.description: Free, cross-platform code editor by Microsoft

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/vscode

debian.install: sudo apt-get install -y wget gpg && wget -qO- https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | gpg --dearmor > packages.microsoft.gpg && sudo install -D -o root -g root -m 644 packages.microsoft.gpg /usr/share/keyrings/microsoft.gpg && echo "deb [arch=amd64,arm64,armhf signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/microsoft.gpg] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/code stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vscode.list && rm -f packages.microsoft.gpg && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y code
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y code

fedora.install:
sudo rpm --import https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc && echo -e "[code]
name=Visual Studio Code
baseurl=https://packages.microsoft.com/yumrepos/vscode
enabled=1
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc" | sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/vscode.repo > /dev/null && sudo dnf install -y code

apps.notes: Microsoft binaries ship telemetry and use the proprietary Marketplace (see VSCodium for FOSS).

apps.name: VS Code Insiders
apps.description: Daily preview build of Visual Studio Code

windows.install: scoop bucket add versions; scoop install versions/vscode-insiders

debian.install: sudo apt install -y code-insiders
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y code-insiders

apps.notes: Preview/daily channel; installs alongside stable. apt/dnf use the same Microsoft code repo as stable. No Flathub Insiders build.

apps.name: VSCodium
apps.description: Free/Libre, telemetry-free binaries of VS Code

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/vscodium

debian.install: wget -qO - https://gitlab.com/paulcarroty/vscodium-deb-rpm-repo/raw/master/pub.gpg | gpg --dearmor | sudo dd of=/usr/share/keyrings/vscodium-archive-keyring.gpg && echo 'deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/vscodium-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.vscodium.com/debs vscodium main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vscodium.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y codium
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y codium

fedora.install:
sudo rpmkeys --import https://gitlab.com/paulcarroty/vscodium-deb-rpm-repo/raw/master/pub.gpg && printf "[gitlab.com_paulcarroty_vscodium_repo]
name=download.vscodium.com
baseurl=https://download.vscodium.com/rpms/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=1
repo_gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://gitlab.com/paulcarroty/vscodium-deb-rpm-repo/raw/master/pub.gpg
metadata_expire=1h
" | sudo tee -a /etc/yum.repos.d/vscodium.repo && sudo dnf install -y codium

apps.notes: Community FOSS build of VS Code; defaults to Open VSX marketplace. Command is codium.

apps.name: VSCodium Insiders
apps.description: Insiders/preview build of VSCodium
apps.notes: Insiders channel distributed only via GitHub releases (vscodium-insiders repo); no winget/choco/scoop/apt/dnf/flatpak/snap packages.

apps.name: Cursor
apps.description: AI-first code editor (VS Code fork) by Anysphere

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/cursor

universal.install: download the official AppImage from https://cursor.com/download, chmod +x and run; it self-updates in place.

apps.notes: Proprietary AI editor built on VS Code; Linux ships as an AppImage. No maintained Chocolatey package.

apps.name: Zed
apps.description: High-performance, multiplayer code editor written in Rust
apps.notes: Open-source. No maintained Chocolatey/Scoop/apt/dnf/snap; install script or Flathub on Linux.

apps.name: Google Antigravity
apps.description: Google's agentic AI IDE (agent-first development platform)

windows.install: download the x64/ARM64 installer from https://antigravity.google/download

debian.install: add Google's Antigravity apt repo + key per https://antigravity.google/download, then sudo apt install antigravity
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y antigravity

fedora.install: add Google's Antigravity dnf repo per https://antigravity.google/download, then sudo dnf install antigravity

apps.notes: Free AI IDE from Google (GA 2026). Requires a personal Google account and Chrome.

apps.name: Antigravity IDE
apps.description: Antigravity IDE desktop build (separate brew cask)

debian.install: sudo apt install antigravity
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y antigravity

apps.notes: Sibling brew cask to antigravity for the same Google product; new product, confirm IDs at install time.

apps.name: Devin Desktop
apps.description: Agentic IDE / agent command center by Cognition (successor to Windsurf)

windows.install: download the Windows installer from https://devin.ai/download

universal.install: download the Linux build (AppImage / archive) from https://devin.ai/download

apps.notes: Cognition's rebranded Windsurf IDE; shipping brew cask is devin-desktop@next (beta). Proprietary; requires sign-in.

apps.name: Kiro
apps.description: AWS agentic, spec-driven IDE (VS Code-based)

universal.install: download the Linux build (.deb/.tar.gz/AppImage) from https://kiro.dev/downloads/

apps.notes: AWS agentic IDE; no AWS account required to download. winget id Amazon.Kiro should be confirmed (new product).

apps.name: Sublime Text
apps.description: Fast, proprietary cross-platform text/code editor

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/sublime-text

debian.install: wget -qO - https://download.sublimetext.com/sublimehq-pub.gpg | gpg --dearmor | sudo tee /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/sublimehq-archive.gpg > /dev/null && echo "deb https://download.sublimetext.com/ apt/stable/" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/sublime-text.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y sublime-text
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y sublime-text

fedora.install: sudo rpm -v --import https://download.sublimetext.com/sublimehq-rpm-pub.gpg && sudo dnf config-manager --add-repo https://download.sublimetext.com/rpm/stable/x86_64/sublime-text.repo && sudo dnf install -y sublime-text

apps.notes: Proprietary; unlimited free evaluation, license required for continued use. Official apt/dnf repos.

apps.name: Helix
apps.description: Post-modern modal text editor written in Rust

debian.install: sudo add-apt-repository ppa:maveonair/helix-editor && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y helix
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y helix

apps.notes: Terminal/modal editor (Kakoune/Neovim inspired). Binary command is hx.

apps.name: Lapce
apps.description: Fast, open-source code editor written in Rust

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/lapce

apps.notes: Open-source (Apache-2.0); GPU-accelerated. No maintained Chocolatey package.

apps.name: Kate
apps.description: KDE's advanced text editor

debian.install: sudo apt install -y kate
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y kate

apps.notes: Open-source KDE application; native on Linux. No maintained Chocolatey/Scoop on Windows.

apps.name: CotEditor
apps.description: Lightweight plain-text editor for macOS
apps.notes: macOS-only, open-source; also on the Mac App Store.

apps.name: CodeEdit
apps.description: Native macOS code editor written in SwiftUI
apps.notes: macOS-only, open-source, native SwiftUI app.

apps.name: Eclipse IDE
apps.description: Extensible multi-language IDE (Eclipse Platform)

windows.install: scoop bucket add java; scoop install java/eclipse-java

apps.notes: Open-source (EPL). Distro apt/dnf packages are stale; use Eclipse Installer, Flatpak, or Snap. Requires a JDK.

apps.name: NetBeans
apps.description: Apache NetBeans IDE (Java, PHP, JavaScript, C/C++)

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/netbeans

apps.notes: Open-source (Apache-2.0). Requires a JDK. Snap requires --classic.

apps.name: Android Studio
apps.description: Official Android IDE by Google (IntelliJ-based)

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/android-studio

apps.notes: Free; IntelliJ-platform based. Flathub build is community-maintained, not Google's official archive.

apps.name: Notepad++
apps.description: Free Scintilla-based source-code and text editor for Windows
apps.notes: Windows only. No official macOS/Linux build (Wine workaround only).

apps.name: Spring Tools for Eclipse
apps.description: Spring Tools 4 — Spring/Spring Boot tooling for the Eclipse IDE (a.k.a. Spring Tool Suite)
apps.notes: Eclipse plugin / bundled STS distribution; all OS package managers are N/A. brew cask token unconfirmed against Homebrew registry.

apps.name: Claude Code
apps.description: Anthropic's agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal
apps.notes: Proprietary. npm package is officially deprecated; prefer the native install script (auto-updates, zero deps).

apps.name: Claude
apps.description: Anthropic's Claude desktop application (GUI chat client)
apps.notes: Desktop chat app, distinct from the Claude Code CLI. No official Linux build.

apps.name: Codex
apps.description: OpenAI Codex CLI — lightweight coding agent that runs in your terminal
apps.notes: Open source CLI. Use scoped package @openai/codex; the unscoped codex npm package is unrelated.

apps.name: Codex App
apps.description: OpenAI Codex desktop application (GUI workspace for Codex)
apps.notes: Proprietary desktop app, separate from the Codex CLI. macOS + Windows only.

apps.name: Gemini CLI
apps.description: Google's open-source Gemini AI agent for the terminal
apps.notes: Open source (Apache-2.0). Requires Node.js 20+. Verify command is gemini.

apps.name: Qwen Code
apps.description: Alibaba's open-source AI coding agent for the terminal (Qwen-based)
apps.notes: Open source. Requires Node.js 20+. Verify command is qwen.

apps.name: Mistral Vibe
apps.description: Mistral's minimal CLI coding agent (Devstral-powered) for the terminal
apps.notes: Open source (Apache-2.0). Python 3.12+. Windows is partial (works but not primary target).

apps.name: OpenCode
apps.description: Open-source AI coding agent built for the terminal
apps.notes: Open source. npm package is opencode-ai (not opencode). Cannot use Anthropic Claude models directly.

apps.name: OpenCode Desktop
apps.description: OpenCode desktop GUI client (graphical companion to the OpenCode agent)

windows.install: scoop bucket add extras; scoop install extras/opencode-desktop

debian.install: sudo dpkg -i opencode-desktop_*.deb; sudo apt-get install -f

universal.install: Direct .deb / .rpm from https://opencode.ai/download or GitHub releases

apps.notes: Open source; desktop app is in beta. No Flatpak/Snap; Linux is .deb/.rpm only.

apps.name: Kiro CLI
apps.description: AWS Kiro CLI — agentic AI coding agent for the terminal (formerly Amazon Q Developer CLI)

debian.install: wget https://desktop-release.q.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/latest/kiro-cli.deb && sudo dpkg -i kiro-cli.deb && sudo apt-get install -f

apps.notes: AWS product. No official Homebrew package despite brewType CASK; use vendor scripts. Windows requires Windows 11.

apps.name: Aider
apps.description: AI pair-programming in your terminal, with Git-aware editing
apps.notes: Open source (Apache-2.0). No official Homebrew formula. PyPI app package is aider-chat. Requires Python 3.10-3.12.

apps.name: Block Goose CLI
apps.description: Block's open-source, extensible AI agent (Goose) — CLI

windows.install: Run the official install script inside WSL: curl -fsSL https://github.com/block/goose/releases/download/stable/download_cli.sh | bash

apps.notes: Open source (Apache-2.0). CLI binary is goose. Windows is partial (WSL); desktop GUI is separate cask block-goose.

apps.name: Antigravity CLI
apps.description: Google DeepMind Antigravity CLI — Go-based terminal coding agent (Antigravity 2.0 harness)
apps.notes: Very new (2026) Go-based TUI; successor to Gemini CLI. Binary is agy. No confirmed Homebrew/winget/npm package.

apps.name: Cursor CLI
apps.description: Cursor's terminal coding agent (cursor-agent) — run Cursor agents in the terminal/CI
apps.notes: Proprietary; CLI is in beta. Binary is cursor-agent. Native Windows install via WSL/Git Bash.

apps.name: Devin CLI
apps.description: Cognition's Devin CLI — local coding agent that can hand off sessions to Devin's cloud
apps.notes: Proprietary (Cognition AI), released April 2026. No confirmed Homebrew/winget/npm/pip package; vendor scripts only.

apps.name: IINA
apps.description: Free, open-source media player for macOS built on mpv.
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build.

apps.name: Spotify
apps.description: Digital music streaming service desktop client.

debian.install: curl -sS https://download.spotify.com/debian/pubkey_C85668DF69375001.gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor --yes -o /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/spotify.gpg && echo "deb https://repository.spotify.com stable non-free" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/spotify.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y spotify-client
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y spotify-client

apps.notes: Proprietary. No official Fedora/RHEL rpm repo — use Flatpak or Snap.

apps.name: VLC Media Player
apps.description: Free, cross-platform multimedia player and framework.

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y vlc
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y vlc

apps.notes: Open source (GPL). On Fedora/RHEL, vlc lives in RPM Fusion (free), not base repos.

apps.name: Jellyfin
apps.description: Free software media system / client for the open-source Jellyfin server.

debian.install: sudo apt install -y curl gnupg && sudo mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings && curl -fsSL https://repo.jellyfin.org/jellyfin_team.gpg.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/jellyfin.gpg && echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/jellyfin.gpg] https://repo.jellyfin.org/$( . /etc/os-release; echo $ID )/$( . /etc/os-release; echo $VERSION_CODENAME ) $( . /etc/os-release; echo $VERSION_CODENAME ) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/jellyfin.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y jellyfin
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y jellyfin

fedora.install: sudo curl -fsSL https://repo.jellyfin.org/jellyfin.repo -o /etc/yum.repos.d/jellyfin.repo && sudo dnf install -y jellyfin

apps.notes: The brew jellyfin cask and apt/dnf repos are the server; the Flathub package is the client (Media Player).

apps.name: FFmpeg
apps.description: Complete cross-platform solution to record, convert, and stream audio and video.

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y ffmpeg
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y ffmpeg

apps.notes: Open source (LGPL/GPL). Fedora base repos ship a stripped ffmpeg-free; full build needs RPM Fusion free.

apps.name: foobar2000
apps.description: Advanced freeware audio player for Windows and macOS.
apps.notes: Freeware (proprietary). No native Linux build — only the Windows version under Wine.

apps.name: Audacious
apps.description: Lightweight, advanced audio player focused on low resource use.

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y audacious
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y audacious

apps.notes: Open source. No official macOS build; no published winget package.

apps.name: mpv
apps.description: Free, open-source, and cross-platform command-line media player.

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y mpv
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y mpv

apps.notes: Open source (GPL/LGPL). The winget entry is the mpv.net GUI fork; for vanilla mpv on Windows use Scoop or Chocolatey.

apps.name: HandBrake
apps.description: Open-source tool for converting video from nearly any format to a selection of modern codecs.

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y handbrake handbrake-cli
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y handbrake

apps.notes: Open source (GPL). The snap is community-published as handbrake-jz. Fedora package names are capitalized.

apps.name: LibreOffice
apps.description: Free and open-source office suite (successor to OpenOffice.org).

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y libreoffice
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y libreoffice

apps.notes: Open source (MPL 2.0). Choco offers libreoffice-fresh (latest) and libreoffice-still (LTS); block uses Fresh.

apps.name: OpenOffice
apps.description: Apache OpenOffice — open-source office productivity suite.
apps.notes: Open source (Apache 2.0). No package-manager support on Linux; manual tarball install only. Consider LibreOffice.

apps.name: SoftMaker FreeOffice
apps.description: Free office suite by SoftMaker (Word/Excel/PowerPoint-compatible).

debian.install: wget -qO- https://shop.softmaker.com/repo/linux-repo-public.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/softmaker.gpg && echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/softmaker.gpg] https://shop.softmaker.com/repo/apt stable non-free" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/softmaker.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y softmaker-freeoffice-2024
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y softmaker-freeoffice-2024

fedora.install: sudo rpm --import https://shop.softmaker.com/repo/linux-repo-public.key && sudo dnf install -y softmaker-freeoffice-2024

apps.notes: Freeware (proprietary; free registration key required). Year-suffix (2024) tracks current release.

apps.name: WPS Office
apps.description: Office suite by Kingsoft (Writer/Spreadsheets/Presentation), MS-Office compatible.

debian.install: download .deb from https://www.wps.com/download then sudo apt install -y ./wps-office_*.deb
debian.update: re-download newer .deb and sudo apt install -y ./wps-office_*.deb

fedora.install: download .rpm from https://www.wps.com/download then sudo dnf install -y ./wps-office-*.rpm
fedora.update: re-download newer .rpm and sudo dnf install -y ./wps-office-*.rpm

apps.notes: Proprietary (free tier with ads). No official apt/dnf repo. Flatpak and snap are community-maintained.

apps.name: SumatraPDF
apps.description: Lightweight reader for PDF, ePub, MOBI, CHM, XPS, DjVu, and comic books
apps.notes: Free, open-source (GPLv3). Windows only — no macOS/Linux build (Wine only).

apps.name: Blender
apps.description: Free and open-source 3D creation suite (modeling, animation, rendering, compositing, video).

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y blender
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y blender

apps.notes: Open source (GPL). The Blender snap needs classic confinement.

apps.name: Upscayl
apps.description: Free and open-source AI image upscaler.

debian.install: download .deb from https://github.com/upscayl/upscayl/releases then sudo apt install -y ./upscayl-*.deb
debian.update: re-download newer .deb and sudo apt install -y ./upscayl-*.deb

fedora.install: download .rpm from https://github.com/upscayl/upscayl/releases then sudo dnf install -y ./upscayl-*.rpm
fedora.update: re-download newer .rpm and sudo dnf install -y ./upscayl-*.rpm

apps.notes: Open source (AGPL). No official apt/dnf repo; use standalone .deb/.rpm/AppImage or Flathub.

apps.name: Mochi Diffusion
apps.description: Run Stable Diffusion natively on Mac using Apple's Core ML.
apps.notes: macOS only — uses Apple Core ML; best on Apple Silicon. No Windows or Linux build.

apps.name: Diffusion Bee
apps.description: Easiest way to run Stable Diffusion locally on macOS — no setup required.
apps.notes: macOS only — no supported Windows or Linux build. Optimized for Apple Silicon.

apps.name: Draw Things
apps.description: Run Stable Diffusion to create images locally on Apple devices.
apps.notes: macOS / iOS only — distributed primarily via the App Store. Confirm MAS id before scripting.

apps.name: PCSX2
apps.description: Free and open-source PlayStation 2 (PS2) emulator.

universal.install: https://pcsx2.net/downloads (official Linux .AppImage — chmod +x PCSX2-*.AppImage then run)

apps.notes: Open source (GPL). No official apt/dnf package on Linux — use AppImage or Flathub. Requires a legal PS2 BIOS dump.

apps.name: Neovim
apps.description: Hyperextensible Vim-based text editor

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y neovim
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y neovim

apps.notes: Distro apt packages often lag upstream; Flatpak binary is not on PATH by default (use flatpak run io.neovim.nvim).

apps.name: Nano
apps.description: Free and friendly replacement for the Pico text editor

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y nano
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y nano

apps.notes: No first-party winget package; preinstalled on most Linux/macOS systems.

apps.name: Git
apps.description: Distributed revision control system

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y git
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y git

apps.notes: macOS may use Apple's bundled git; distro packages can lag upstream (use ppa:git-core/ppa on Ubuntu).

apps.name: Vim
apps.description: Vi 'workalike' with many additional features

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y vim
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y vim

apps.notes: On minimal Debian/Ubuntu only vim-tiny is present; on Fedora/RHEL the full package is vim-enhanced.

apps.name: JEnv
apps.description: Manage your Java environment (per-shell/dir JDK switching)
apps.notes: macOS/Linux only, no native Windows build; not packaged in apt/dnf (use git clone).

apps.name: nvm
apps.description: Node Version Manager — POSIX-compliant shell function to manage Node versions
apps.notes: Windows uses the unrelated nvm-windows project (not API-compatible: nvm version vs nvm --version).

apps.name: PyEnv
apps.description: Python version management
apps.notes: Windows uses the separate pyenv-win project; building Python on Unix requires toolchain/headers.

apps.name: uv
apps.description: Extremely fast Python package and project manager
apps.notes: uv self update only works for Astral-script installs, not Homebrew/winget/distro builds.

apps.name: tree
apps.description: Display directories as trees (with optional color/HTML output)

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y tree
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y tree

apps.notes: Windows has a built-in tree.com (limited); the GNU port id may vary by source (GnuWin32.Tree).

apps.name: tldr-pages (tlrc)
apps.description: Official tldr-pages client written in Rust

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y tlrc
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y tlrc

apps.notes: Invoked command is tldr, not tlrc; apt/dnf availability depends on distro version (else use cargo).

apps.name: jq
apps.description: Lightweight and flexible command-line JSON processor

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y jq
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y jq

apps.notes: Project moved to the jqlang org (hence winget id jqlang.jq).

apps.name: sd
apps.description: Intuitive find & replace CLI (sed alternative)

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y sd
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y sd

apps.notes: The winget id chmln.sd should be confirmed against the registry before generation if exact-id matching is strict.

apps.name: ripgrep
apps.description: Recursively search directories for a regex pattern (rg)

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y ripgrep
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y ripgrep

apps.notes: Invoked command is rg, not ripgrep; use the .MSVC winget variant on Windows.

apps.name: fd
apps.description: Simple, fast, user-friendly alternative to find

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y fd-find
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y fd-find

apps.notes: On Debian/Ubuntu the package is fd-find and the binary is fdfind (symlink to fd); on Fedora binary is fd; cargo crate is fd-find.

apps.name: midnight-commander (mc)
apps.description: Terminal-based visual file manager (mc)

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y mc
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y mc

apps.notes: Windows support is limited to MSYS2/Cygwin; no first-party winget/choco/scoop package.

apps.name: ranger
apps.description: VI-keyed terminal file manager with previews

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y ranger
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y ranger

apps.notes: No native Windows build (depends on Unix curses, use WSL); PyPI/pipx package is ranger-fm while command is ranger.

apps.name: nnn
apps.description: Tiny, fast, feature-packed terminal file manager

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y nnn
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y nnn

apps.notes: No native Windows package (use WSL); version check is nnn -V (capital V).

apps.name: lazygit
apps.description: Simple terminal UI for git commands

debian.install: sudo add-apt-repository ppa:lazygit-team/release && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y lazygit
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y lazygit

fedora.install: sudo dnf copr enable atim/lazygit -y && sudo dnf install -y lazygit

apps.notes: Not in default Debian/Ubuntu repos (use the PPA); Fedora uses the atim/lazygit COPR.

apps.name: bat
apps.description: Cat clone with syntax highlighting and Git integration

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y bat
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y bat

apps.notes: On Debian/Ubuntu the binary installs as batcat (symlink to bat); on Fedora/cargo/winget the command is bat.

apps.name: RTK
apps.description: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption (a.k.a. "Rust Token Killer")
apps.notes: Not in winget/choco/scoop/apt/dnf; install via Homebrew tap, install.sh, GitHub releases, or cargo --git (bare crate name rtk may pull an unrelated crate). Younger project — verify tap name and release assets before generation.

apps.name: AWS CLI
apps.description: Official Amazon AWS command-line interface

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y awscli
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y awscli

apps.notes: v1 vs v2: official installers/Homebrew/winget/Chocolatey give v2; many distro apt/dnf and PyPI/pipx packages give v1 (prefer the official v2 bundle).

apps.name: Postman
apps.description: Collaboration platform for API development
apps.notes: No official apt/dnf repo — on Linux use Flatpak, Snap, or the official tarball; Flathub build is community-maintained.

apps.name: Bruno
apps.description: Open-source, offline-first API client (Git-friendly, no cloud account)

debian.install: sudo mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings && sudo gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /etc/apt/keyrings/bruno.gpg --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9FA6017ECABE0266 && echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/bruno.gpg] https://debian.usebruno.com/ bruno stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/bruno.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y bruno
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y bruno

apps.notes: Official apt repo exists; for Fedora/RHEL use the .rpm, Flatpak, or Snap (no dnf repo); @usebruno/cli is the headless runner, not the GUI.

apps.name: Transmission
apps.description: Lightweight, cross-platform BitTorrent client

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y transmission-gtk
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y transmission-gtk

apps.notes: Pick the matching Linux front-end: transmission-gtk (GNOME) vs transmission-qt (KDE); the Snap is community-maintained.

apps.name: Wget
apps.description: Internet file retriever (non-interactive HTTP/HTTPS/FTP downloader)

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y wget
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y wget

apps.notes: GNU.Wget2 is the newer wget2 rewrite; JernejSimoncic.Wget packages classic GNU wget. Not preinstalled on macOS.

apps.name: cURL
apps.description: Command-line tool and library for transferring data with URLs

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y curl
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y curl

apps.notes: Already present on Windows 10/11, macOS, and most Linux; on macOS Homebrew's curl is keg-only. Removing distro curl can pull dependencies.

apps.name: Android File Transfer
apps.description: Transfers files from and to an Android smartphone
apps.notes: macOS only; do not confuse with the unrelated open-source android-file-transfer-linux (whoozle) project.

apps.name: OpenMTP
apps.description: Advanced Android file transfer application for macOS
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build is published.

apps.name: AppCleaner
apps.description: Application uninstaller
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build.

apps.name: Keka
apps.description: File archiver
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build; also sold on the Mac App Store (paid).

apps.name: ExifTool
apps.description: Perl lib for reading and writing EXIF metadata

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y libimage-exiftool-perl
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y libimage-exiftool-perl

apps.notes: Package names differ: Debian libimage-exiftool-perl, Fedora perl-Image-ExifTool, Arch perl-image-exiftool; Windows is a self-contained binary.

apps.name: Pinentry (pinentry-mac)
apps.description: Pinentry for GPG on Mac
apps.notes: macOS-only variant; Linux uses distro pinentry/pinentry-gtk2/curses/qt, Windows uses the pinentry bundled with Gpg4win.

apps.name: Pearcleaner
apps.description: Application uninstaller and leftover-file cleaner
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build.

apps.name: htop
apps.description: Improved top (interactive process viewer)

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y htop
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y htop

apps.notes: No native Windows build — use WSL and install via apt/dnf there.

apps.name: nvtop
apps.description: Interactive GPU process monitor

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y nvtop
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y nvtop

apps.notes: Linux-focused GPU monitor, no native Windows build (use WSL); macOS Apple GPU support is newer — verify against hardware.

apps.name: iTerm2
apps.description: Terminal emulator as alternative to Apple's Terminal app
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build (use Windows Terminal on Windows).

apps.name: QLMarkdown (sbarex)
apps.description: Quick Look generator for Markdown files
apps.notes: macOS only — depends on the macOS Quick Look framework; no Windows or Linux equivalent.

apps.name: OnyX
apps.description: Verifies system files structure, runs maintenance and cleaning tasks
apps.notes: macOS only; each OnyX version targets one macOS release (Homebrew serves the build for your current OS).

apps.name: Maintenance
apps.description: Operating system maintenance and cleaning utility
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build (a simplified subset of OnyX's maintenance tasks).

apps.name: Deeper
apps.description: Personalization utility to configure hidden options of macOS
apps.notes: macOS only — no Windows or Linux build (OS-version-specific build like OnyX/Maintenance).

apps.name: CalHash
apps.description: Computes checksums/hashes of files
apps.notes: macOS only; equivalent CLIs exist elsewhere (sha256sum, certutil -hashfile).

apps.name: 7-Zip
apps.description: High-ratio file archiver (creates/extracts 7z, zip, tar, and more)

debian.install: sudo apt install -y 7zip
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y 7zip

apps.notes: Official GUI is Windows-only; macOS/Linux get the 7zz console tool. Package names differ: 7zip provides 7zz, p7zip/p7zip-full provide 7z.

apps.name: TopNotch
apps.description: Blacks out the menu bar to hide the MacBook notch
apps.notes: macOS only — the notch is MacBook hardware, so there is no Windows or Linux build.

apps.name: Docker Desktop
apps.description: App to build and share containerized applications and microservices

debian.install: download `docker-desktop-<arch>.deb` from https://docs.docker.com/desktop/setup/install/linux/ then `sudo apt install -y ./docker-desktop-*.deb`
debian.update: download the newer `.deb` and re-run `sudo apt install -y ./docker-desktop-*.deb`

fedora.update: download the newer `.rpm` and re-run `sudo dnf install -y ./docker-desktop-*.rpm`

apps.notes: Proprietary (subscription required for larger orgs). Bundles the Docker Engine, CLI, Compose v2, and a GUI. On Linux it runs the engine inside a managed VM.

apps.name: Docker (Engine / CLI)
apps.description: Pack, ship and run any application as a lightweight container

debian.install: sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo tee /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc >/dev/null && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo $VERSION_CODENAME) stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list >/dev/null && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin
debian.remove: sudo apt remove -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin

fedora.install: sudo dnf -y install dnf-plugins-core && sudo dnf config-manager --add-repo https://download.docker.com/linux/fedora/docker-ce.repo && sudo dnf install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin && sudo systemctl enable --now docker
fedora.update: sudo dnf upgrade -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin
fedora.remove: sudo dnf remove -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin

apps.notes: On macOS, `brew install docker` installs only the CLI client (no daemon — pair it with Docker Desktop, Colima, or a remote engine). The full daemon lives on Linux. On Windows the engine is delivered via Docker Desktop running in WSL 2; scoop installs CLI only.

apps.name: Docker Compose
apps.description: Define and run multi-container applications with Docker

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y docker-compose-plugin
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y docker-compose-plugin

apps.notes: Compose v2 is a Docker CLI plugin invoked as `docker compose`. The legacy v1 `docker-compose` standalone binary is end-of-life. On Linux install via Docker's official apt/dnf repo so the plugin version matches the engine.

apps.name: Podman
apps.description: Tool for managing OCI containers and pods

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y podman
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y podman

apps.notes: Apache-2.0, daemonless, rootless-capable. On macOS/Windows it runs containers in a managed Linux VM (`podman machine`). On macOS run `podman machine init && podman machine start` after install. Podman Desktop is a separate GUI product.

apps.name: Podman Compose
apps.description: Script to run docker-compose.yml using Podman

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y podman-compose
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y podman-compose

apps.notes: Python implementation of Compose for Podman. On Windows install via `pipx install podman-compose` (or `pip install podman-compose`); requires Python. Modern Podman can also run Compose files natively via `podman compose`.

apps.name: UTM
apps.description: Virtual machines UI using QEMU
apps.notes: Apache-2.0 QEMU front-end for macOS (and iOS) only — no Windows or Linux build. Also on the Mac App Store (paid convenience build); the GitHub/website .dmg is free. Use plain QEMU/virt-manager on Linux.

apps.name: DBeaver Community
apps.description: Universal database tool and SQL client

debian.install: curl -fsSL https://dbeaver.io/debs/dbeaver.gpg.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/dbeaver.gpg && echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/dbeaver.gpg] https://dbeaver.io/debs/dbeaver-ce /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/dbeaver.list >/dev/null && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y dbeaver-ce
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y dbeaver-ce

fedora.install: download the official `.rpm` from https://dbeaver.io/download/ then `sudo dnf install -y ./dbeaver-ce-*.rpm`
fedora.update: download the newer `.rpm` and re-run `sudo dnf install -y ./dbeaver-ce-*.rpm`

apps.notes: Apache-2.0 (Community Edition). Java-based; bundled JRE in most installers (the tarball may need a system JDK). DBeaver PRO is a separate commercial product.

apps.name: DataGrip
apps.description: Database IDE from JetBrains
apps.notes: Proprietary, paid (subscription) JetBrains IDE. No apt/dnf/Flathub packaging — distributed through JetBrains' own channels. On Linux use Snap (`--classic`, unconfined), the JetBrains Toolbox App, or the official tarball.

apps.name: Draw.io (drawio-desktop)
apps.description: Diagrams.net desktop app to create and edit diagrams

debian.install: download `drawio-amd64-<ver>.deb` from https://github.com/jgraph/drawio-desktop/releases then `sudo apt install -y ./drawio-*.deb`
debian.update: download the newer `.deb` and re-run `sudo apt install -y ./drawio-*.deb`

fedora.update: download the newer `.rpm` and re-run `sudo dnf install -y ./drawio-*.rpm`

apps.notes: Apache-2.0 Electron app (formerly diagrams.net / mxGraph). Fully offline. The Flatpak run name is `com.jgraph.drawio.desktop`; the binary is not on PATH by default.

apps.name: Tad
apps.description: Desktop application for viewing and analyzing tabular data

debian.install: download `tad_<ver>_amd64.deb` from https://github.com/antonycourtney/tad/releases then `sudo apt install -y ./tad_*.deb`
debian.update: download the newer `.deb` and re-run `sudo apt install -y ./tad_*.deb`

fedora.install: download the official `.rpm` from https://github.com/antonycourtney/tad/releases then `sudo dnf install -y ./tad-*.rpm`
fedora.update: download the newer `.rpm` and re-run `sudo dnf install -y ./tad-*.rpm`

apps.notes: MIT-licensed Electron app (antonycourtney/tad); views CSV, Parquet, SQLite and DuckDB files. No winget/Chocolatey/Scoop/Flathub/Snap packages — install from GitHub releases or the Homebrew cask. Arch has the community AUR package `tad-bin`. Download the build matching your CPU architecture.

apps.name: ProtonVPN
apps.description: VPN client for secure internet access

debian.install: wget https://repo.protonvpn.com/debian/dists/stable/main/binary-all/protonvpn-stable-release_1.0.8_all.deb && sudo dpkg -i ./protonvpn-stable-release_1.0.8_all.deb && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y proton-vpn-gnome-desktop
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y proton-vpn-gnome-desktop

fedora.install: sudo dnf install -y https://repo.protonvpn.com/fedora-$(awk -F= '/^VERSION_ID/{print $2}' /etc/os-release | tr -d '"')-stable/protonvpn-stable-release/protonvpn-stable-release-1.0.3-1.noarch.rpm && sudo dnf install -y proton-vpn-gnome-desktop

apps.notes: Freemium. Linux install is two-step: install Proton's `protonvpn-stable-release` repo package, then `proton-vpn-gnome-desktop`. The version numbers in the repo-package URLs change over time — pull the current value from https://protonvpn.com/support/official-linux-vpn/. The Flathub entry `com.protonvpn.www` is community-maintained, not an official Proton release.

apps.name: LM Studio
apps.description: Discover, download, and run local LLMs

universal.install: Download the AppImage from https://lmstudio.ai/download (x86_64), then `chmod +x LM-Studio-*.AppImage && ./LM-Studio-*.AppImage`. The app self-updates in place. Remove by deleting the AppImage.

apps.notes: Proprietary, free for personal use. macOS build is Apple-Silicon only (no Intel build). Linux is x86_64 AppImage only — no apt/dnf/flatpak/snap and no ARM Linux build. Bundles a `lms` command-line helper (enable with `lms bootstrap`). A `ElementLabs.LMStudio.Portable` winget id also exists for the portable build. libfuse2 may be required for AppImages on some distros.

apps.name: Ollama (Desktop App)
apps.description: Run, create, and share large language models (desktop app)
apps.notes: The desktop app, distinct from the headless CLI/server entry. On macOS (>= 14) and Windows the app bundles the `ollama` CLI and server — installing it gives you both the GUI and the `ollama` command (don't also install the `ollama` Homebrew formula on macOS). App self-updates. A `Ollama.Ollama.Portable` winget id exists for the portable build. On Linux there is no desktop-app cask — use the Ollama (CLI) install script.

apps.name: Ollama (CLI)
apps.description: Run, create, and share large language models (CLI + server)

universal.remove: sudo systemctl stop ollama && sudo systemctl disable ollama && sudo rm /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service && sudo rm $(which ollama)

apps.notes: Headless CLI/server, distinct from the desktop app. On Linux the official install script is canonical — installs the `ollama` binary to /usr/local/bin and creates a systemd service `ollama.service`. Manual install: download the platform `.tgz` from https://ollama.com/download/linux. Homebrew on Linux (`brew install ollama`) also works. On macOS/Windows the desktop app already bundles this CLI — don't install both the cask and the formula on macOS. The Windows winget package `Ollama.Ollama` is the same artifact as the desktop entry.

apps.name: Llama.cpp
apps.description: LLM inference in C/C++ (the ggml/llama.cpp engine and tools)

universal.install: git clone https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp && cd llama.cpp && cmake -B build [-DGGML_CUDA=ON|-DGGML_VULKAN=ON|-DGGML_HIP=ON] && cmake --build build --config Release -j

apps.notes: Open source (MIT), maintained by ggml-org. Primarily distributed via Homebrew formula, winget, release binaries, and build-from-source; there is no official apt/dnf/flatpak/snap. Binaries are prefixed `llama-` (llama-cli, llama-server, llama-quantize) — there is no single `llama.cpp` command. Pre-built release binaries (CPU/CUDA/Vulkan/HIP/Metal): https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases. `llama-cpp-python` (PyPI) is a separate Python binding, not this engine.

apps.name: Joplin
apps.description: Free, open source note-taking and to-do application
apps.notes: Open source (MIT/AGPL components). Desktop app self-updates. The official Linux desktop path is the AppImage install script; the Flathub (`net.cozic.joplin_desktop`) and snap (`joplin-desktop`) builds are community-maintained. A separate terminal app (npm package `joplin`) exists; Joplin docs recommend the `NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX=~/.joplin-bin` install. No official Chocolatey/Scoop package on Windows — winget is canonical.

apps.name: MarkEdit
apps.description: Just like TextEdit on Mac but dedicated to Markdown
apps.notes: Open source (GPL-3.0), macOS only — a native Swift/AppKit Markdown editor. Available via Homebrew cask `markedit` and the Mac App Store (same app). Requires a recent macOS (>= 15). No Windows or Linux version.

apps.name: Amazon Corretto
apps.description: No-cost, production-ready distribution of OpenJDK from Amazon (with LTS)

windows.install: scoop bucket add java; scoop install java/corretto{version}-jdk

debian.install: wget -O - https://apt.corretto.aws/corretto.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/corretto-keyring.gpg && echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/corretto-keyring.gpg] https://apt.corretto.aws stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/corretto.list && sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y java-{version}-amazon-corretto-jdk
debian.update: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install --only-upgrade -y java-{version}-amazon-corretto-jdk

fedora.install: sudo rpm --import https://yum.corretto.aws/corretto.key && sudo curl -L -o /etc/yum.repos.d/corretto.repo https://yum.corretto.aws/corretto.repo && sudo dnf install -y java-{version}-amazon-corretto-devel

apps.notes: Free AWS long-term support. All five majors (8, 11, 17, 21, 25) available. apt package schema is `java-{version}-amazon-corretto-jdk`; yum/dnf uses `…-devel`. SDKMAN!: `sdk install java {version}.x.y-amzn`. Deep JAVA_HOME / multi-JDK guidance in java.md.

apps.name: Eclipse Temurin (Adoptium)
apps.description: Vendor-neutral OpenJDK build from Eclipse Adoptium

windows.install: scoop bucket add java; scoop install java/temurin{version}-jdk

debian.install: sudo apt install -y wget apt-transport-https gpg && wget -qO - https://packages.adoptium.net/artifactory/api/gpg/key/public | gpg --dearmor | sudo tee /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/adoptium.gpg > /dev/null && echo "deb https://packages.adoptium.net/artifactory/deb $(awk -F= '/^VERSION_CODENAME/{print$2}' /etc/os-release) main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/adoptium.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y temurin-{version}-jdk
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y temurin-{version}-jdk

fedora.install:
cat <<'EOF' | sudo tee /etc/yum.repos.d/adoptium.repo
[Adoptium]
name=Adoptium
baseurl=https://packages.adoptium.net/artifactory/rpm/fedora/$releasever/$basearch
enabled=1
gpgcheck=1
gpgkey=https://packages.adoptium.net/artifactory/api/gpg/key/public
EOF
sudo dnf install -y temurin-{version}-jdk

apps.notes: Most widely used free, vendor-neutral build. All five majors (8, 11, 17, 21, 25) available. apt/dnf package schema is `temurin-{version}-jdk`. On Linux Mint substitute UBUNTU_CODENAME for VERSION_CODENAME. SDKMAN!: `sdk install java {version}.x.y-tem`.

apps.name: Microsoft Build of OpenJDK
apps.description: Microsoft's no-cost LTS distribution of OpenJDK

windows.install: scoop bucket add java; scoop install java/microsoft{version}-jdk

debian.install: register the Microsoft repo for your distro (see learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/install), then: sudo apt install -y msopenjdk-{version}
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y msopenjdk-{version}

fedora.install: register the Microsoft yum repo, then `sudo dnf install -y msopenjdk-{version}`

apps.notes: No Java 8 build exists — only 11/17/21/25 (Microsoft is the exception that lacks 8). Best for Azure / legacy 17/21 workloads. Scoop's manifest schema is `microsoft{version}-jdk` (NOT `microsoft-openjdk…`). Java 25 build confirmed 2026-06-11, but verify availability for very fresh PSU releases before generating. SDKMAN!: `sdk install java {version}.x.y-ms`.

apps.name: Azul Zulu
apps.description: Azul's certified OpenJDK build (broad platform/version matrix)

windows.install: scoop bucket add java; scoop install java/zulu{version}-jdk

debian.install: sudo apt install -y gnupg ca-certificates curl && curl -s https://repos.azul.com/azul-repo.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/azul.gpg && echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/azul.gpg] https://repos.azul.com/zulu/deb stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/zulu.list && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y zulu{version}-jdk
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y zulu{version}-jdk

fedora.install: sudo rpm --import https://repos.azul.com/azul-repo.key && sudo curl -L -o /etc/yum.repos.d/zulu.repo https://repos.azul.com/zulu/rpm/zulu.repo && sudo dnf install -y zulu{version}-jdk

apps.notes: Widest version/arch coverage of the four. All five majors (8, 11, 17, 21, 25) available. Azul apt/dnf package schema is `zulu{version}-jdk` (e.g. zulu21-jdk). SDKMAN!: `sdk install java {version}.x.y-zulu`.

apps.name: Node.js
apps.description: JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine

debian.install: curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_{version}.x | sudo -E bash - && sudo apt-get install -y nodejs
debian.update: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install --only-upgrade -y nodejs

fedora.install: curl -fsSL https://rpm.nodesource.com/setup_{version}.x | sudo bash - && sudo dnf install -y nodejs

apps.notes: `node@22` / `node@24` pin LTS lines; `{version}` is a major (22 or 24). For version management prefer nvm or fnm. Distro `nodejs` is often old — use the NodeSource `setup_{version}.x` repo or nvm/fnm. The unpinned latest ids are `OpenJS.NodeJS` (winget), `nodejs` (choco/scoop). On Debian distro `npm` is a separate package; NodeSource bundles npm.

apps.name: Python
apps.description: Python programming language interpreter

debian.install: sudo add-apt-repository -y ppa:deadsnakes/ppa && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y python{version} python{version}-venv
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y python{version}

apps.notes: `{version}` is a minor like 3.13. For project/interpreter management prefer uv or pyenv. The apt/dnf package is `python{version}` (e.g. python3.13); deadsnakes PPA is needed on Debian/Ubuntu for minors not in the distro. Choco meta-pkg `python` tracks latest 3.x; scoop versioned manifests exist (e.g. python313). Don't break the system python3 — install extra minors side-by-side or use uv/pyenv.

apps.name: Go
apps.description: The Go programming language compiler and toolchain

debian.install: sudo add-apt-repository -y ppa:longsleep/golang-backports && sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y golang-go
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y golang-go

apps.notes: Single unpinned entry (tracks latest stable). Distro `golang-go` lags upstream — the official go.dev/dl tarball is the recommended way to get the current release; longsleep PPA is the apt alternative. `go install <pkg>@latest` installs tools once Go is present. GOPATH / GOROOT / module detail in go.md.

apps.name: Maven
apps.description: Java build / dependency-management tool (Apache Maven)

debian.install: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y maven
debian.update: sudo apt update && sudo apt install --only-upgrade -y maven

apps.notes: Maven installs only the binary — it picks its JDK via JAVA_HOME and does not install one. Prefer the committed wrapper `./mvnw` per project. SDKMAN!: `sdk install maven`. Maven 4.0 is not GA as of 2026-06-11 (3.9.x is stable). Category BUILD_TOOLING.
